skillName: Find the nth term of a sequence
misconceptionSummary: Students follow a procedure for nth term without understanding why the difference becomes the coefficient of n.

visuals.ruleText: In a linear sequence, the common difference becomes the coefficient of n
visuals.subText: Because each new position adds one more copy of that difference

steps.narration: That is why the coefficient in the nth term comes from the common difference.
Four n grows by four each time, so it matches the step size of the sequence.

lines.text: 4n may not start at the correct first term

steps.narration: The adjustment changes the starting point while keeping the same growth rate.
The plus three shifts the 4n pattern into the right place.

visuals.label: Growth rate and starting point both matter

steps.narration: The conceptual structure of an nth term is step size plus starting adjustment.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: memorising a method without understanding the roles of difference and adjustment. The difference controls growth; the adjustment fixes the starting position.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Think of the nth term as two parts: one for how fast it grows, and one for where it starts.
